title: Just-in-time detection of silent security patches
reason: The paper introduces a novel machine learning approach, llmda, for detecting silent security patches, which is clearly related to the topic of Machine Learning for Code. It leverages large language models to enhance patch information and employs a new method, PT-Former, for aligning and fusing multi-modal embeddings. The paper evaluates the approach on two datasets, PatchDB and SPI-DB, demonstrating the impact of ML solutions in software engineering. The contributions align with the desired topic, making it relevant for inclusion.

title: Can LLMs Reason About Program Semantics? A Comprehensive Evaluation of LLMs on Formal Specification Inference
reason: The paper introduces a new benchmark, FormalBench, specifically designed to evaluate the reasoning abilities of Large Language Models (LLMs) on program semantics, which is directly related to the application of machine learning in coding tasks. title: Why Personalizing Deep Learning-Based Code Completion Tools Matters

title: EnCus: Customizing Search Space for Automated Program Repair
reason: The paper focuses on a novel approach called EnCus for customizing the search space in automated program repair (APR) using an ensemble-based method. title: Towards detecting prompt knowledge gaps for improved llm-guided issue resolution

title: Search-based llms for code optimization
